results.requirement: Participant count is between 2 and 19 when tournament is active or finished
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_ParticipantCount(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures m.phase != Setup ==> 2 <= |m.participants| <= 19
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: When the model is not in the Setup phase, the number of participants is between 2 and 19 (inclusive).
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: Requirement says 'active or finished'; lemma uses 'phase != Setup'. Assuming the only phases are Setup, Active, and Finished, these are equivalent. The count bounds [2, 19] are preserved exactly.

results.requirement: Total rounds is at least 1 when tournament is active or finished
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_TotalRoundsMin(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures m.phase != Setup ==> m.totalRounds >= 1
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: When the model is not in the Setup phase, the total number of rounds is at least 1.
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: Same phase encoding as req 0. 'phase != Setup' is equivalent to 'active or finished'. The bound (totalRounds >= 1) matches the requirement exactly.

results.requirement: Current round count never exceeds total rounds
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_CurrentRoundBound(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures CurrentRound(m) <= m.totalRounds
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: The current round of the tournament never exceeds the total number of rounds.
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: The postcondition CurrentRound(m) <= m.totalRounds holds unconditionally for all invariant states, matching 'never exceeds' in the requirement.

results.requirement: Participants have non-negative scores at all times
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_ScoresNonNegative(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures forall i :: 0 <= i < |m.participants| ==> m.participants[i].score2x >= 0
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: All participant scores (represented as twice their actual score) are non-negative.
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.postcondition: For all participant indices i in the model, m.participants[i].score2x ≥ 0.
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: score2x >= 0 for all participants correctly encodes non-negative scores. The doubled representation is an implementation detail; the semantic meaning is preserved.

results.requirement: Opponent tracking is symmetric across all participants (FIDE Rule 2 foundation)
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_OpponentsSymmetric(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures OpponentsSymmetric(m.participants)
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: The opponent relationships among participants are symmetric (if participant A played participant B, then B played A).
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: The lemma delegates to OpponentsSymmetric(m.participants) for all invariant states. The back-translation correctly explains this as symmetry of opponent tracking.

results.requirement: Accepted round pairings contain no rematches between participants (FIDE Rule 2)
results.dafnyCode:
lemma NoRematchesWhenValid(m: Model, pairings: seq<Pairing>, bye: Option<nat>)
    requires ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)
    ensures NoRematchPairings(m.participants, pairings)
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: When pairings and a bye are valid for a model, those pairings contain no rematches (no two participants are paired more than once).
informalization.preconditions: The pairings and bye form valid pairings for the model (ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)).
informalization.scope: A single state with specific pairings.

comparison.explanation: The lemma proves NoRematchPairings(m.participants, pairings) whenever ValidPairingsForModel holds, directly capturing the 'no rematches in accepted pairings' requirement.

results.requirement: Each participant appears at most once in accepted round pairings (FIDE Rule 3)

informalization.naturalLanguage: In valid pairings, every participant appears in at most one pairing, either as white or black.
informalization.preconditions: The pairings and bye form valid pairings for the model (ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)).
informalization.postcondition: For all distinct pairing indices i and j, the four participants in those pairings (white and black from each) are all different.
informalization.scope: A single state with specific pairings.

comparison.explanation: The postcondition explicitly states that for all distinct pairing indices i < j, the four participant slots (white/black in each) are all different, ensuring each participant appears at most once.

results.requirement: A bye is present when and only when the participant count is odd (FIDE Rule 3)
results.dafnyCode:
lemma ByeIffOddWhenValid(m: Model, pairings: seq<Pairing>, bye: Option<nat>)
    requires ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)
    ensures bye.Some? <==> |m.participants| % 2 == 1
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: In valid pairings, a bye is assigned if and only if the number of participants is odd.
informalization.preconditions: The pairings and bye form valid pairings for the model (ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)).
informalization.scope: A single state with specific pairings.

comparison.explanation: The biconditional bye.Some? <==> |m.participants| % 2 == 1 exactly captures 'bye present when and only when participant count is odd'.

results.requirement: Accepted bye recipient has not previously received a pairing-allocated bye (FIDE Rule 4)
results.dafnyCode:
lemma ByeRecipientEligibleWhenValid(m: Model, pairings: seq<Pairing>, bye: Option<nat>)
    requires ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)
    requires bye.Some?
    ensures !m.participants[bye.value].byeReceived
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: When a bye is assigned in valid pairings, the recipient has not previously received a bye.
informalization.preconditions: The pairings and bye form valid pairings for the model, and a bye exists (bye.Some?).
informalization.postcondition: The participant at index bye.value has not received a bye before: !m.participants[bye.value].byeReceived.
informalization.scope: A single state with specific pairings where a bye is present.

comparison.explanation: !m.participants[bye.value].byeReceived directly encodes that the bye recipient has not previously received a bye. The precondition bye.Some? correctly scopes this to cases where a bye is assigned.

results.requirement: Active round always has structurally valid pairings and valid participant indices
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_ActiveRoundValid(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures ActiveRoundValid(m)
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: The active round of the model satisfies validity constraints.
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: The lemma proves ActiveRoundValid(m) for all invariant states. The predicate encapsulates both structural validity of pairings and valid participant indices per the requirement. Delegating to a named predicate is standard in this codebase and not a weakening.

results.requirement: Color history length equals total games played as white plus black for every participant
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_ColorHistoryConsistency(m: Model)
    requires Inv(m)
    ensures forall i :: 0 <= i < |m.participants| ==>
      |m.participants[i].colorHistory| == m.participants[i].whiteCount + m.participants[i].blackCount
  {}

informalization.naturalLanguage: For each participant, the length of their color play history equals the sum of their white and black play counts.
informalization.preconditions: The model satisfies the invariant Inv(m).
informalization.postcondition: For all participant indices i, |m.participants[i].colorHistory| == m.participants[i].whiteCount + m.participants[i].blackCount.
informalization.scope: All reachable states (states satisfying the invariant).

comparison.explanation: The postcondition |colorHistory| == whiteCount + blackCount for all participants exactly matches the requirement's statement about color history length equaling total games played.

results.requirement: When colors are assigned via ChooseColors, the color difference for each participant in the pairing stays within minus 2 to 2 (FIDE Rule 6)

informalization.naturalLanguage: When assigning colors to two participants via ChooseColors, the resulting color difference for each participant (adjusted by their assigned color) remains within the acceptable range of [-2, 2].
informalization.preconditions: Two different participant indices p1idx and p2idx; both participants have color difference in [-2, 2]; no conflicting hard constraints on both players (not both must play black, not both must play white, no player forced to play both colors).
informalization.postcondition: After ChooseColors assigns one player white and one black, both players' adjusted color differences remain in [-2, 2]: -2 ≤ ColorDiff(p1) ± 1 ≤ 2 and -2 ≤ ColorDiff(p2) ± 1 ≤ 2.

comparison.explanation: The lemma proves that ChooseColors preserves ColorDiff within [-2, 2] for both participants. Preconditions that both start within [-2, 2] and have no conflicting hard constraints are legitimate guard conditions for a preservation lemma. The postcondition captures the adjusted ColorDiff (after adding ±1 for the assigned color) remaining in [-2, 2].

results.requirement: When a valid non-repeating color is appended to a participant's color history, the no-three-consecutive-same-color property is preserved (FIDE Rule 7)

informalization.naturalLanguage: Appending a color to a history that has no three consecutive identical colors preserves the no-three-consecutive property, provided the appended color does not create three in a row.
informalization.preconditions: The history has no three consecutive identical colors; either the history has fewer than 2 elements, or the last two elements are not both equal to the color being appended.
informalization.postcondition: After appending color c to history, there are still no three consecutive identical colors in the resulting sequence.

comparison.explanation: The precondition correctly encodes 'valid non-repeating color' (not creating three in a row), and the postcondition proves NoThreeConsecutive is preserved on appending. Fully matches the requirement.

results.requirement: All accepted pairings cover every participant exactly once (bye or paired)
results.dafnyCode:
lemma Guarantee_FullCoverage(m: Model, pairings: seq<Pairing>, bye: Option<nat>)
    requires ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)
    ensures 2 * |pairings| + (if bye.Some? then 1 else 0) == |m.participants|
    ensures forall i, j :: 0 <= i < j < |pairings| ==>
      pairings[i].whiteIdx != pairings[j].whiteIdx &&
      pairings[i].whiteIdx != pairings[j].blackIdx &&
      pairings[i].blackIdx != pairings[j].whiteIdx &&
      pairings[i].blackIdx != pairings[j].blackIdx
    ensures bye.Some? ==>
      bye.value < |m.participants| &&
      (forall p :: p in pairings ==> p.whiteIdx != bye.value && p.blackIdx != bye.value)
  {
    FullCoverageWhenValid(m, pairings, bye);
    PairingsDistinctWhenValid(m, pairings, bye);
  }

informalization.naturalLanguage: Valid pairings fully cover all participants: the number of participants equals twice the number of pairings plus one if a bye exists; all pairings are distinct; and if a bye exists, it is a valid participant index that does not appear in any pairing.
informalization.preconditions: The pairings and bye form valid pairings for the model (ValidPairingsForModel(m, pairings, bye)).
informalization.postcondition: Three conditions hold: (1) 2*|pairings| + (1 if bye exists else 0) == |m.participants|; (2) all pairings are distinct (no participant appears in multiple pairings); (3) if a bye exists, bye.value is a valid index and does not appear in any pairing.
informalization.scope: A single state with specific pairings.

comparison.explanation: The three ensures clauses together prove full coverage: (1) participant count equals 2*pairings + bye, (2) all pairings are distinct (no participant in two pairings), (3) if a bye exists it is a valid index not appearing in any pairing. Together these guarantee every participant is covered exactly once.
